summary:
The death toll from the magnitude-7.8 earthquake that struck the southern Philippines on Monday has risen to at least 37, with hundreds injured. The quake hit about 20 kilometers off the coast of Sarangani province early in the morning, collapsing buildings and triggering tsunami alerts across Mindanao. Aftershocks continue, and officials are warning people not to enter damaged structures. Tremors were felt as far away as Manado, Indonesia, 420 kilometers from the epicenter. Rescue operations are ongoing, and the full extent of the damage is still being assessed.

The Pentagon added several major Chinese companies to its 1260H blacklist on Tuesday, designating them as national-security risks due to alleged military ties. The list includes tech giants Alibaba and Baidu, carmaker BYD, drug maker WuXi AppTec, robot company Unitree, and automaker Nio. The annual update also removed some Chinese firms that no longer operate in the US. This move widens the competition between the world's two largest economies and could affect trade and investment flows. Separately, Israel launched new strikes in southern Lebanon on Tuesday, a day after both Israel and Iran pulled back from direct confrontation. The Israeli campaign against Hezbollah has complicated US-Iran peace talks.

The Philippines accused China of building an artificial structure at the disputed Scarborough Shoal in the South China Sea. Manila's National Task Force for the West Philippine Sea said aerial monitoring spotted a floating platform six meters wide and six meters long, with an antenna and individuals on board. This escalates tensions in the region, where multiple countries have competing claims. Meanwhile, Xi Jinping wrapped up his first official trip to North Korea since 2019, pledging stronger ties with Kim Jong Un but making no public mention of denuclearization, signaling a potential policy shift. In eastern Congo, an Ebola outbreak has reached at least 100 deaths out of 515 confirmed cases across three provinces, less than a month after authorities declared it.

In health news, recent studies suggest that GLP-1 weight loss drugs, such as Ozempic and Wegovy, may protect against cancer beyond their weight loss effects, acting on brain and metabolic pathways to prevent cancer and slow its progression. On the economic front, a surge in global AI investment helped China's exports beat forecasts, offsetting the impact of Middle East turmoil on trade. And in the Ukraine war, drones launched by both Russia and Ukraine are increasingly veering off course into NATO neighbors Lithuania and Romania, raising risks for non-combatant countries.

headlines.title: Philippines earthquake death toll rises to 37 as aftershocks continue
headlines.summary: A magnitude-7.8 earthquake off Sarangani province killed at least 37 people and injured hundreds, collapsing buildings and triggering tsunami alerts in Mindanao.
